title: Who wants EoE on the big screen
display_title: Who wants EoE on the big screen
wikitext:
[]: Who wants EoE on the big screen?             Quote Post by konfunoodle » Tue Mar 09, 2010 10:51 am I know that the possibilities of this happening is basically non-existant, but I guess thats one reason why forums exist. As Rebuild far from appearing in theaters on American shores, I would like to have the pleasure of watching at least one Eva movie on the big screen. As its Evangelion, I'm sure fans of the series will pay to see it in theatres, knowing that we spent money on some merchandise of theirs. The biggest problem I see this happening is that it would most likely be flamed by many for being too "warped" on many different levels and would only be viewed as a treat for the fans. This is due to Gaijin understanding of such works of art. On the optimistic side, it could spark intrest to people unfamiliar with the series(not likely, but still) and this will no doubt be another way to spread the word of the New Beginning Gospel :] Plus, Avatar was an animated movie that somewhat got people to take animation a little more seriously. Now if it were to make it in US theatres, should it be dubbed or subbed? Feel free to put in what Eva movie you would like to see in theatres

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Mar 09, 2010 11:09 am Alongside 2001 there's no other film I want to see on big screen more badly

[]:             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Tue Mar 09, 2010 11:20 am id be more likely to see it if it were live action, i own it on DVD at home, sooo, why pay to see it.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Mar 09, 2010 11:27 am You know, some theatre chains provide rental services: http://www.landmarktheatres.com/theatre_rentals_frm.htm You could always try organizing a special screening in your local region. All you would need is permission from the distributor, they're often fairly reasonable with things like this, especially if you do it through an existing anime club or society, and get the point across that this is not about profit, but sharing an experiences with your peers. I've considered doing something like this in the past. And with the possibility of a higher quality release of EoE, I've thought about it again recently.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Mar 09, 2010 11:36 am The Eva Monkey wrote: You know, some theatre chains provide rental services: http://www.landmarktheatres.com/theatre_rentals_frm.htm You could always try organizing a special screening in your local region. All you would need is permission from the distributor, they're often fairly reasonable with things like this, especially if you do it through an existing anime club or society, and get the point across that this is not about profit, but sharing an experiences with your peers.

[]:             Quote Post by konfunoodle » Tue Mar 09, 2010 1:13 pm supershinjiasukashipper wrote: id be more likely to see it if it were live action, i own it on DVD at home, sooo, why pay to see it.

[]:             Quote Post by Uriel Septim VII » Tue Mar 09, 2010 2:56 pm Just imagine Shinji's ear-shattering screen in high definition surround sound. Giant Naked Rei gets BIG. Was it originally even in theaters, or did it go straight to Video/DVD?

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Mar 09, 2010 3:03 pm Uriel Septim VII wrote: Just imagine Shinji's ear-shattering screen in high definition surround sound. Giant Naked Rei gets BIG. Was it originally even in theaters, or did it go straight to Video/DVD?

[]:             Quote Post by backseatjesus » Tue Mar 09, 2010 3:25 pm Will EOE ever get a Bluray release?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Mar 09, 2010 5:30 pm Xard wrote: Alongside 2001 there's no other film I want to see on big screen more badly

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Tue Mar 09, 2010 8:35 pm I personally can't imagine EoE airing at anything but an anime festival, but I am definitely for the theater rental idea. I love driving down to NC, and I know plenty of people in the Wilmington area. If we can get a showing, I'm there. In 9-10 hours.

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Tue Mar 09, 2010 9:28 pm backseatjesus wrote: Will EOE ever get a Bluray release?

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Tue Mar 09, 2010 10:37 pm the real question is trying to find an actual theatrical print and adding subtitles to it

[]:             Quote Post by backseatjesus » Tue Mar 09, 2010 11:04 pm ran1 wrote: I personally can't imagine EoE airing at anything but an anime festival, but I am definitely for the theater rental idea. I love driving down to NC, and I know plenty of people in the Wilmington area. If we can get a showing, I'm there. In 9-10 hours.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Mar 09, 2010 11:37 pm backseatjesus wrote: ran1 wrote: I personally can't imagine EoE airing at anything but an anime festival, but I am definitely for the theater rental idea. I love driving down to NC, and I know plenty of people in the Wilmington area. If we can get a showing, I'm there. In 9-10 hours.

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Wed Mar 10, 2010 12:13 am except Manga Entertainment doesn't have the rights anymore (Or in the USA at least from what i've read) but yeah. Personally, the only true way to actually experience a film in the theaters is by watching a genuine theatrical print (And yes...I forgot about Death...though we could just splice Rebirth out and splice EoE in)

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Mar 10, 2010 9:52 am GasmaskAvenger wrote: Personally, the only true way to actually experience a film in the theaters is by watching a genuine theatrical print (And yes...I forgot about Death...though we could just splice Rebirth out and splice EoE in)

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Mar 10, 2010 9:57 am FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: Which brings up the question, where could we find a carrier of the Eva film prints?

[]:             Quote Post by Zeruel » Wed Mar 10, 2010 12:35 pm I would definitely drive down to Raleigh to see it and also have a few friends that would come (we live in Northern Virginia) but for me I could settle on a dvd copy or the zx end of evangelion renewal transfers as long as it was in the english dub though. I don't agree with some of the the things Amanda Wen added and the adr staff added in but Tristan Mcavery as Gendo, Spike Spencer as Shinji and others are just to memorable to not listen to again seeing how as Manga Ent. lost the rights and will never see the original cast of dubs released on dvd again.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Mar 10, 2010 12:55 pm Zeruel wrote: ...as long as it was in the english dub though.

As Rebuild far from appearing in theaters on American shores, I would like to have the pleasure of watching at least one Eva movie on the big screen. As its Evangelion, I'm sure fans of the series will pay to see it
